column13: wwWallet is an innovative approach to digital identity wallets, combining the benefits of edge wallets, installed on personal devices, and web/cloud wallets accessible through web browsers. wwWallet has demonstrated interoperability with the EUDI RI Wallet and was selected as one of the four finalists in the SPRIN-D EUDI Wallet Prototype competition. It incorporates unique features such as multi-device support, support for zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs), and native integration with WebAuthn/CTAP and roaming WSCDs.

column13: Procivis focuses primarily on the creation and management of credentials (issuing PIDs, MdLs, QEAA, all types of credentials, all formats, and standards). In addition to the main functionality of providing credentials, we also offer credential verification and storage features via mobile and server-based wallets accessible via SDK or directly via API. Our main mission is to enable national governments, public institutions and service providers to deliver eIDAS 2.0 conformant services on a large scale. We define Procivis One as a robust technology capable of supporting all elements of the digital identity lifecycle (schema definition, design, issuance, storage, revocation, suspension, updating and verification of digital identities) and integrating them into business processes through powerful SDKs and APIs. Although we also provide applications for end users (Holder (Wallet) & Verifier App), our clients can create their own wallets apps , retrieve code of our apps and integrate it into their solutions as a white label. On that basis One of our clients, a service provider, has recently completed a pilot with a public institution trialing our Business Wallet in a real business case environment where the usage of API�s was central & beneficial for a rapid integration.
column14: We would like to support the Wallet group with advice on efficient use of technologies in the Business use cases. Our expertise on Standards and requirements for interoperability also beyond eIDAS could be of benefit for the Wallet group. We are available for contribution to technical subjects and use of technology for performant production environments rather than aspects of business semantics or definition of new to be created standards.
column15: Members of the Procivis staff have been involved in Blockchain and digital ID projects in Germany and Switzerland in Leading Architect, Development and Project Management roles and can share the experience form these publicly funded projects (next to projects with private enterprises).

column13: We can offer a complete wallet suite that includes both a Holder Wallet for natural persons, and a Server Wallet, composed of a Connector and a Business Wallet, designed for legal entities and organizations. The Holder Wallet enables users to store and present credentials on their own behalf. It also supports the issuance of a photo ID-style credential, where a natural person can scan their national ID and capture a selfie to obtain a credential containing their basic identification data, acting as a form of PID. The Server Wallet allows organizations to issue and verify credentials for natural persons. It also enables organizations to receive and store legal credentials (e.g., an IBAN credential). This wallet can be deployed on-premise or offered as a service, and requires integration with the organization�s source of truth in order to retrieve the data needed to issue credentials. It is designed to be business process-agnostic. Limitations: At the moment, our solution does not support selective disclosure. We are happy to support relevant use cases with our tools and expertise, and can provide guidance on wallet integration, credential workflows, and deployment scenarios.
column14: We can contribute our expertise as wallet providers and support both the definition of wallet requirements and aspects related to wallet interoperability. We are also willing and able to attend the WP4 working meetings, preferably if they are scheduled from Monday to Thursday. Additionally, we can provide support with documentation and could assist in coordination tasks as needed.
column15:
In EBSI-VECTOR, we developed the reference server wallet (connector) that enables organizations to issue and receive credentials. We also took part in the education and social security pilots, acting as both server wallet and holder wallet providers. Additionally, we led several work packages and supported coordination activities.

In DC4EU, we contributed by providing our holder wallet, enabling the issuance and verification of credentials in a real-world education pilot scenario. We also participated in the hybrid pilot, which combined the use of digital credentials with certificates as an additional verification mechanism, helping to explore more robust and flexible trust models.

column13: Gataca Wallet is a mature solution currently deployed in production, with around 100,000 active instances and proven ability to sustain high-throughput scenarios of up to 2 million transactions per day. It is developed in React Native with TypeScript, and designed to be interoperable with European standards such as eIDAS 2.0, W3C Verifiable Credentials, OIDC/SIOPv2, and SD-JWT-VC. The wallet natively supports multilingual interfaces (English, Spanish, Portuguese, French, German, Italian, Finnish, and Swedish), secure key management at the OS level, and full compliance with ISO 27001 practices. We have successfully delivered more than four national-scale pilots with governments, proving our ability to operate in highly demanding and regulated environments. In addition, Gataca is deeply involved in EBSI (European Blockchain Services Infrastructure), actively contributing to its evolution and aligning our technology with its trust and interoperability frameworks. In terms of use cases, Gataca Wallet is already supporting diverse deployments, including identity verification, age verification, academic credentials, and enterprise onboarding, with both mobile app distribution for end-users and SaaS/on-premise enterprise wallet deployments. We are committed to ensuring privacy by design, including features such as unique pseudonym generation per relying party, and maintaining full user control of credentials.
column14: Gataca is committed to supporting the Wallets Group in every way possible, leveraging our extensive experience in both technology and real-world use cases. We are fully available to contribute actively to discussions, technical input, and the implementation of pilot activities. However, due to the limited number of person-months (PMs) allocated to our participation, we are not in a position to assume formal leadership roles within the group.
column15: Yes. Gataca has previous experience from LSPs. We participated as observers in the DC4EU project and took an active role in EBSI-VECTOR, where we led Work Package 4 (WP4).
